title: Week in Politics: U.S. turns 250 under record heat; worries over the future
summary: Today's Independence Day celebrations may be tamped down by severe heat and, according to a NPR/PBS News/Marist poll, worry about the future.

title: 'Cautiously optimistic': Swing voters describe their view of America at 250
summary: As America turns 250, voters from our Swing Shift project talk about the state of the country. Their views ranged from "uncertain" and "concerned" to "excited" and "cautiously optimistic."

title: DC parade canceled as excessive heat further upends America 250 festivities
summary: The annual Independence Day parade in Washington, D.C., was canceled late Friday due to sweltering temperatures in the nation’s capital. The event, which is hosted by the National Park Service, was scheduled to begin at 10:30 a.m. on Saturday morning. Organizers said they canceled the procession due to an “Extreme Heat Warning” from the National...

title: Happy Independence Day, America: Your revolution inspired ours
summary: Robert Emmet, the Irish republican leader. said he wished to secure for Ireland “the guarantee which Washington procured for America."

title: ‘It is a crisis’: Putin under increasing pressure from Ukraine war
summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin is coming under rare public criticism at home, a significant signal of how the pressures from the more than four-year war in Ukraine are hurting the country. Fuel shortages and rising inflation, high-profile attacks against Russian energy infrastructure and cities, and mounting military casualties are prompting prominent figures to start pinning the blame...

title: Kean's depression disclosure prompts questions about mental health stigma
summary: When Rep. Tom Kean Jr. (R-N.J.) disclosed that his monthslong disappearance from the House was due to depression, he opened a delicate but difficult conversation: How much information about their personal health are politicians expected to share? After vanishing from public life for more than 100 days and offering almost no details, Kean returned...

title: 5 cities missing out on hosting FIFA World Cup matches in 2026
summary: Some major U.S. cities are getting an economic boost from the World Cup, with places like Los Angeles, Miami and Dallas flooded with thousands of overseas tourists attending matches to cheer on their home countries and packing bars to take in the action on the pitch. Economists and tourism experts have said the impact of the event on local economies might not be felt for weeks and is best measured on a...

title: Nearly a Million Investors Lost a Total of $3.8 Billion on Trump Crypto Coin
summary: A report from a cryptocurrency analytics firm details how those who bought the Trump memecoin have fared, with most retail investors having lost money while sophisticated traders did better.

title: Readers Pick the Definitive Films That Capture America
summary: Though “Idiocracy” and “The Godfather” topped their list, the sheer range of suggestions shows how there are many ways to understand the country.

title: Representative Joyce Beatty Still Celebrating Victory in Kennedy Center Legal Battle
summary: Representative Joyce Beatty, Democrat of Ohio, said her lawsuit challenging the president’s changes to the performing arts center was a fight for justice.

title: ‘Who Should I Vote for?’ Voters Turn to A.I. Before Casting Their Ballots
summary: It takes effort to be an informed citizen. Artificial intelligence tools offer an alluring shortcut — but they’re not without risk.

title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce in NYC ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
summary: The star-studded celebration saw several streets in Midtown Manhattan shut, as hordes of fans gathered outside the arena.

title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
summary: More than 165 million people are enduring dangerous heat and humidity, with temperatures expected to stay at 38C (100F) for several days.

title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

title: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

summary: The week-long remembrance is intended to demonstrate public support for the regime

title: US clean power prices set to soar as AI demand coincides with subsidy cuts
summary: Clean power purchase agreements, favoured by companies like Google and Meta, expected to rise sharply as Biden-era support ends

title: ‘Heat dome’ over eastern US sends electricity prices soaring
summary: More than 800,000 households left without power after storms and temperatures near 40C straining utility grids

title: Trump uses Mount Rushmore speech to warn of ‘communist menace’ in US
summary: President kicked off 250th anniversary events with a preview of a likely line of attack against Democrats in coming elections

title: America at 250
summary: What does the US see when it looks in the mirror? As a nation of impossible ambition celebrates its founding, a special edition of FT Weekend Magazine asks writers, thinkers and leaders to reflect on the first 250 years

title: The Supreme Court, Mail-in Voting, Campaign Spending, and the 2026 Midterm Elections
summary: The Supreme Court’s decision this week on mail-in ballots got the most attention, but its decision on campaign spending may have a bigger impact on the fight for control of Congress.

title: A Year Later, Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill Act Has a Clean Tech Silver Lining
summary: Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill Act gutted the wind and solar credits at the heart of Biden’s Inflation Reduction Act. But a year on, the law’s preserved incentives for geothermal, advanced nuclear, and grid-scale storage may do more to cut global emissions than the credits it eliminated.

title: Behind the curve: Britain’s defence investment plan in Trump’s world
summary: The new defence investment plan is weighed down by the habits of Britain’s defence establishment. By contrast, the British public appear readier to accept change.

title: Regional roots, global decisions: Andy Burnham’s foreign policy challenge
summary: Andy Burnham, Britain’s likely next prime minister, is clear-eyed about his domestic political priorities. But, with the world order in flux, all eyes are on his foreign policy approach

title: The future is fermented: How Europe can succeed in the next industrial race
summary: The next industrial revolution will be built with microbes as much as machines. China understands this. Europe still has the science to compete but must act quickly before industrial leadership slips away again

title: The China challenge
summary: Mark Leonard welcomes Mike Kuiken and Randy Schriver to examine the evolving US-China relationship and what it means for Europe

title: Ex-Olympian charged over alleged reflecting pool damage
summary: The Trump administration has indicted a former Olympian over damage to the National Mall's reflecting pool. A lawyer for David Hearn says he's innocent.

title: Brooks and Capehart on whether the Supreme Court has stood up to Trump

title: A Belgian diamond group is gifting Trump a lavish ring after that industry won tariff relief
summary: It's a glitzy window into the role that ostentatious – and almost always gilded — gifts are playing by those seeking to curry favor with the U.S. president.

title: The FBI is directing hundreds of analysts to its investigation of Georgia's 2020 presidential election
summary: Georgia's votes in that year's presidential race were counted three times, including once by hand, and each count affirmed Democrat Joe Biden's win.

title: Trump reads a children's book on second lady Usha Vance's podcast, then riffs on past presidents and himself
summary: The president read "Presidents Play!" a children's book from the White House Historical Association that features illustrations of the presidents enjoying sports and using the White House and its grounds for recreation.

title: Marriage Penalties and Bonuses in the Federal Tax Code
summary: When a couple marries, their federal income tax payments are likely to be impacted. Marriage may cause a couple’s tax payments to increase, resulting in a marriage tax penalty, or decrease, resulting in a marriage tax bonus. Marriage penalties and bonuses emerge because of the impossibility of balancing three competing goals in the income tax code: progressivity (higher-income taxpayers pay higher marginal tax...

title: Trump v. Slaughter and the Future of For-Cause Removal Protections
summary: In a 6-3 decision on June 29, 2026, the Supreme Court held that statutory provisions that allow the President to remove Federal Trade Commission (FTC) commissioners only “for cause” violate the Constitution. The Court’s decision, written by Chief Justice Roberts and joined by Justices Thomas, Alito, Gorsuch, Kavanaugh, and Barrett, explicitly overrules the 1935 Supreme Court case Humphrey’s Executor v. United...

title: Washington's Growing Portfolio: Tracking U.S. Government Investments
summary: CFR’s U.S. Government Deal Tracker shows how the government is experimenting with new tools and financing structures to advance a range of strategic sectors, including critical minerals, energy, global logistics, manufacturing, telecommunications, and other technologies.

title: Indra Nooyi Says India's Chaos Is The Cost Of Democracy Compared To China's Model

title: Maine Senate Race Is Tight, With Concerns About Both Candidates
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Daron Shaw said, "Along with North Carolina, Maine is the Democrats’ most obvious Senate target... How do you blow that? Maybe by nominating someone facing allegations of racism and dishonesty. This race could demonstrate whether partisan and populist loyalties trump all else."

title: Keir Starmer Goes All In On ‘new Nuclear Age’ Citing China, Russia And North Korea

title: Teacher Pension Problems Continue To Get Bigger
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Joshua Rauh said, “When you hear framings like ‘schools are underfunded’ or ‘schools are overfunded,’ usually those are missing the point. It’s that a growing share of what we already spend never reaches students, and until that problem is directly confronted, even the largest tax increases will struggle to deliver on their stated promises.”

title: Unconventional deterrence: How Europe can ward off a Russian attack
summary: A nuclear deterrent does not preclude major war. Putin has learnt this lesson—now Europe needs to do the same.

title: Insights from table-top exercises in Europe on AI safety and cyber misuse

title: Farm Bill Primer: Programs Without a Budget Baseline
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) provides budgetary projections of government spending called baselines. Following rules of both House and Senate budget committees, CBO assumes that many farm bill programs that receive mandatory funding will continue beyond the end of their authorization. That is, they have a continuing baseline beyond the end of a farm bill that gives them built-in future funding if Congress...

title: How Schools and After-School Programs Can Collaborate to Support Academic Alignment
summary: The authors provide national data about the state of academic alignment between school-day and after-school learning, illustrate how schools and after-school programs put academic alignment into practice, and identify enabling and impeding factors.

title: Military Effectiveness Should Be Determined by The War Department Not Judges
summary: The military isn't a therapeutic institution; its purpose is to deter war, fight war, and win war. There are hard truths in military life that a humane society may dislike but cannot repeal. The first is that military service is not an entitlement. It is a calling, yes; an honor, certainly. But it is also a profession whose members must be prepared to deploy, fight, endure privation, and, if necessary, die in...

title: America’s Asian Allies Want Nuclear Submarines. Here’s How To Build Them
summary: Nuclear power is cool once again and is all the rage in Asia. The North Koreans are getting in on the action with the rumored development of a nuclear-powered submarine. Not to be outdone, America’s allies, South Korea and Japan , are serious about having the capability for their future submarines. For America, having allies with lethal nuclear-powered submarines will bolster deterrence against China’s massively...

title: Don't Regulate Away America's Medical Device Sterilization Industry
summary: In 2024, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) tried to make it harder for hospitals to obtain essential sterilized medical equipment by threatening to regulate the American industry out of business. Two years later, the EPA is finally safeguarding the medical equipment supply chain by rescinding a damaging and badly conceived rule. The rule centers on a substance known as Ethylene Oxide (EtO). Although it...

title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus
summary: The authors identify the inventory of critical electrical equipment for artificial intelligence (AI) data center operations and the impact of supply chain vulnerabilities on the U.S. power grid’s ability to meet AI demand by 2030.

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.
